ctx.name: Carl
ctx.notes: They are being pulled into two different directions. AZPocket App and the CoE UX/UI

introQueue.label: The real version
introQueue.name: Tell me about your week — the real version.
introQueue.description: Kim Scott's 'care personally' made concrete. Signals that a performed answer won't cut it.

introQueue.label: Agenda check
introQueue.name: Before we get into it, anything you want to make sure we cover today?
introQueue.description: Semi-set early question. Gives the report explicit permission to set the agenda before the manager's plan takes over.

introQueue.label: Friction scan
introQueue.name: What's been slower or harder than it should have been?
introQueue.description: Invites them to name blockers without framing it as complaint.

introQueue.label: Opening pace check
introQueue.name: How's the last two weeks actually felt — energy-wise?
introQueue.description: Opener that invites a real answer rather than 'fine'. Reads wellbeing directly.

focus_points.label: Choosing between AZPocket and CoE UX/UI priorities.
focus_points.reason: Notes say Carl is being pulled into two different directions. One possibility is competing priorities across AZPocket App and the CoE UX/UI workstream—worth clarifying what he’s saying yes to, what’s slipping, and what tradeoff he wants to make.

focus_points.label: What’s stuck between the two workstreams.
focus_points.reason: Notes imply a split focus; could be dependencies or unclear decision ownership between AZPocket App and the CoE UX/UI. Worth identifying the specific dependency, decision, or coordination friction that keeps him from moving both forward cleanly.

focus_points.label: What he wants more of from you.
focus_points.reason: Whether he needs air cover, faster decisions, or clearer prioritization from you so he can stop context-switching and protect time for the work that matters most right now.

focus_points.label: How he’s keeping stakeholders in sync.
focus_points.reason: At UX Lead in a bi-weekly check-in, stakeholder alignment is the default risk reducer: making sure both the AZPocket App stakeholders and the CoE UX/UI stakeholders understand timelines, tradeoffs, and what’s changing—so surprises don’t accumulate between meetings.

brief.coreIssue: This bi-weekly check-in is likely about helping a UX Lead make explicit tradeoffs between AZPocket App and CoE UX/UI demands.
brief.openingQuestion: What tradeoff between AZPocket and CoE UX/UI feels most important to make explicit right now so you can lead both responsibly?
listenFor: whether he names one workstream first and treats the other as reactive or vague
whether he points to a specific decision owner, stakeholder, or dependency that is slowing both sides
if they describe what they need from you in concrete terms like priority calls, air cover, or stakeholder alignment
avoid: do not treat this like a personal time-management problem if the real issue is unclear ownership or competing asks
do not leave the meeting with both workstreams still framed as equally urgent without naming the tradeoff
brief.goodOutcome: You and Carl have agreed which workstream gets lead-level attention first and what decision or stakeholder boundary you will reinforce so he can protect that scope.
brief.suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example where AZPocket and CoE UX/UI competed for Carl’s time so you can test the tradeoff with something concrete.

queueRef.label: Opening pace check
queueRef.name: How's the last two weeks actually felt — energy-wise?
queueRef.description: Opener that invites a real answer rather than 'fine'. Reads wellbeing directly.

queueRef.label: Friction scan
queueRef.name: What's been slower or harder than it should have been?
queueRef.description: Invites them to name blockers without framing it as complaint.

queueRef.label: Decision pressure
queueRef.name: Where are the two workstreams asking you to make the hardest tradeoff?
queueRef.description: Surfaces the specific point where split focus is forcing a choice.

queueRef.label: Stakeholder sync
queueRef.name: Who needs the clearest update from you this week?
queueRef.description: Checks where stakeholder alignment matters most and whether anyone is at risk of being surprised.

queueRef.label: Protect the work
queueRef.name: What are you choosing to protect time for next?
queueRef.description: Forces a prioritization call and reveals what he is intentionally keeping alive.

queueRef.label: Next two weeks
queueRef.name: What would make the next two weeks feel cleaner for you?
queueRef.description: Closes on practical relief and a near-term adjustment rather than a new topic.

history.answer_excerpt: Its been okay, we had team building and now new OE's are coming on board its great
